acceptance.assertion: The adapter accepts one reviewed merge commit, verifies its tree and remote-main binding, creates a clean detached versioned release checkout, installs frozen dependencies, builds the runtime and records exact source and artifact identities before switching services.

leitstand.service and leitstand-storage-health.service are generated from reviewed templates or typed configuration, switched as one bounded transaction, and automatically restored to the previous verified release when any post-switch assertion fails.

Local and canonical HTTPS health, active and removed route matrices, permanent RepoGround redirect, listener ownership, process cwd, unit working directories, storage-producer result and clean release status are verified with structured parsers rather than fragile raw-header matching.

Each attempt writes an immutable receipt with old and new release IDs, unit hashes, health and route evidence, outcome classification and rollback evidence; an already completed identical deployment is detected without repeating effects.
Runbooks, operator catalog and repository entry points identify the actual user-systemd production path and clearly distinguish it from optional Docker/Compose or static-preview paths.

Automated tests cover pre-switch failure, unit-generation drift, health failure, route-readback failure, producer failure, automatic rollback and verification-script false negatives such as CRLF-sensitive redirect parsing.

A reviewed merged release is deployed through the adapter, exact commit and tree are live, all required snapshots are within their source-specific freshness limits, removed routes remain 404, and the prior release remains available as bounded rollback evidence.

approval.note: User authorized autonomous task completion on 2026-07-19; exact live state, isolated worktree, diff-bound review, green CI, merge and fail-closed deployment readback remain mandatory.

goal: Implement and adopt one first-class, receipt-bound deployment adapter for immutable Leitstand user-systemd releases so exact source identity, build, coupled unit switching, rollback and live readbacks are reproducible instead of reconstructed ad hoc.

does_not_establish: implementation before owner-repository PR and merge
permission to alter foreign dirty checkouts or units
successful deployment without exact post-effect readbacks
metadata.observed_failure: The first safe rollout attempt restored the prior release because a raw HTTP-header regex mishandled CRLF despite healthy service and route behavior.

title: Leitstand versioned user-systemd deployment adapter
